name: gh-737-full-suite-string-scrape-misfire
description: run-tests.sh ignored its positional file arg so 'pnpm test <entry>' ran the whole suite; a sibling test's TITLE contained the literal string MODULE_NOT_FOUND and the stdout-scraping load-failure guard rejected a genuine assertion-only RED as structurally broken. The agent's work was correct throughout.

baseRun.notes: whole-suite run; entry-scoped signal drowned in prose output

headRun.notes: runner not scoped to the entry file; test count unavailable; stdout contains a passing test title with the literal string MODULE_NOT_FOUND

expected.rationale: The work is fine; the only defect is observational (unscoped run, prose scraping). Absence of clean scoped evidence must advance with a flag, never block — the old gate wedged here.

provenance.notes: With a structured JSON reporter this same boundary is VERIFIED (fail-on-base, pass-on-head, count>0). Fixture pins the UNVERIFIED-not-CONTRADICTED behavior for stdout-scrape ambiguity.
